patch.value: GH-611: delete/modify merge silently resurrects deleted items and leaves conflict markers in history/*.jsonl while validate stays green

Post-merge validate detects a live .toon whose history contains a delete op (resurrection candidate) and history files containing git conflict markers, failing with remediation hints; documented delete-vs-edit merge policy states which side wins and how to honor the losing side; repro from GH-611 becomes a regression test

author: maintainer-agent
message: link history-stream owner: delete-op semantics live there

patch.value: GH-611. pm delete removes .toon but appends a delete op to history/<id>.jsonl (file kept). Delete-on-A vs edit-on-B merge: git delete/modify resolution keeps B's .toon → deleted item silently resurrects; history left with conflict markers; validate ok:true. Fix direction: validate flags live .toon whose history ends in delete op (resurrection candidate) + conflict-marker scan in history files; document delete-vs-edit merge policy.
